description: A village or well at the end of a journey takes 'kau'. Phi's allative makes that landmark the promised point of arrival. 'wea' (TOWARD) supplies direction without that promise. 'mua' (LOC) marks where an event occurs, so 'kamo' (arrive) takes 'mua' rather than 'kau'.
articulatory_notes: Press the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release it into open central 'a'. Keep the hiatus clear while raising the back of the tongue and rounding the lips for high-back 'u'. Stress 'ka'.
usage_notes: The goal comes immediately after 'kau', and the whole phrase occupies the ordinary adjunct position before any direct object. 'lue' (ABL) supplies the source at the other end of the path.
